vitals.terrain: Mixed: singletrack, beaches, dirt roads, gravel roads, sealed roads, cycle lanes, trails, paths, and five boat trips
vitals.field_size: 750+ riders (2020); waves of up to 100 people per day
vitals.location: Cape Reinga to Bluff, New Zealand

course_description.overview: Tour Aotearoa is New Zealand's longest bikepacking event, stretching 3,000km from Cape Reinga at the northernmost tip to Bluff at the southern coast. The route follows a combination of cycle trails, tracks, paths and lanes connected by country roads, designed to highlight the best New Zealand Cycle Trail Great Rides, Heartland Rides, and quiet backcountry roads. The route includes five boat trips and traverses diverse terrain from subtropical beaches and volcanic plateaus to alpine passes and rugged southern plains.

key_segments.name: Ninety Mile Beach
key_segments.description: Coastal section requiring low tide navigation; start dates selected to maximize time for completion at low tide

key_segments.name: New Zealand Cycle Trail Great Rides
key_segments.description: Sections incorporating the country's premier cycle trails

key_segments.name: Backcountry Roads
key_segments.description: Quiet country roads connecting major trail systems

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Tour Aotearoa presents a multi-faceted challenge combining extreme distance (3,000km) with diverse and variable terrain. Riders encounter everything from technical singletrack and beach riding to gravel and sealed roads, with five boat crossings adding logistical complexity. The self-supported format requires complete autonomy in navigation, gear management, and resupply. While not technically extreme, the combination of distance, terrain variety, and self-sufficiency requirement creates a substantial endurance and mental challenge.

logistics.getting_there: Riders must arrange their own transportation to Cape Reinga, New Zealand's northernmost point. International participants typically fly into New Zealand and arrange ground transport to the start.
logistics.accommodation: Self-supported brevet format; riders are responsible for all accommodation, food, and logistics throughout the journey. A fully supported commercial alternative is available through Track and Trail Cycle Adventures, which provides accommodation, food, water crossings, laundry, Wi-Fi, and e-bike charging.
logistics.registration_details: Registration for 2026 opens soon on touraotearoa.nz. Brevet participants must make a $100 donation to a charity of their choice and offset carbon emissions from travel. Start dates in 2026 are February 19, 26, 27, and 28, with staggered start times (7:00 am, 8:00 am, 9:00 am respectively) to optimize Ninety Mile Beach low tide conditions.
logistics.bike_logistics: Self-supported format requires riders to carry or resupply all gear. The route includes five boat trips, which riders must navigate independently. GPS-based route tracking available at ta26.maprogress.com.
logistics.local_considerations: Riders must be prepared for New Zealand's variable weather and terrain. Five boat crossings are required as part of the official route. The event emphasizes sustainability and environmental respect. Tide timing at Ninety Mile Beach is critical and influences official start dates.

final_verdict.one_liner: An iconic, self-supported bikepacking odyssey across New Zealand's entire length that demands complete autonomy, endurance, and adventure spirit.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced bikepacking adventurers seeking a major self-supported challenge with exceptional scenery and cultural significance. Ideal for riders comfortable with navigation, self-sufficiency, and variable terrain. International cyclists seeking a bucket-list bikepacking experience. Those motivated by charity fundraising and environmental consciousness.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Absolutely, for the right rider. Tour Aotearoa offers one of the world's premier bikepacking experiences with notable New Zealand scenery, a well-designed route, and a thriving community. The self-supported format and charity component add meaningful depth. However, the high logistics burden, significant expense, and complete self-reliance requirement make it unsuitable for casual cyclists. The 30-day time limit is achievable but demanding.
pro_tips: Plan your resupply strategy meticulously; self-supported means you own all logistics completely
Start on February 26-28 to benefit from optimal Ninety Mile Beach low tide timing
Choose your charity early and consider fundraising beyond the required $100 donation
Use ta26.maprogress.com to track your progress and stay connected with the community
Prepare for terrain variety: pack versatile gear suitable for singletrack, beaches, gravel, and sealed roads
Offset your carbon emissions as requested; the event emphasizes environmental responsibility
